⚠ MELANOTAN SAFETY. MT-II can suppress appetite and cause nausea, flushing, and mole darkening. Monitor moles — get a skin check every 6 months. MT-I is much cleaner but won't affect libido. Neither replaces sunscreen.
TESTOSTERONE — YOUR SETUP
Daily 7x/wk EOD 3-4x/wk M/W/F 3x/wk M/Th 2x/wk Weekly 1x/wk
Daily = most stable (pen-friendly) · EOD = 3-4 pins/wk · 2x/wk = standard syringe protocol

⚠ TESTOSTERONE REQUIRES A PRESCRIPTION
Testosterone is a Schedule III controlled substance in the US. You MUST have a valid prescription and a licensed provider monitoring bloodwork (Total T, Free T, Estradiol, CBC, PSA) every 90 days. This tool is for tracking only — it does not constitute medical advice. If your provider hasn't run baseline labs, stop and get them done first.
🚨
STOP — CRITICAL INFO
Are you using acetic acid to reconstitute your AOD-9604?
AOD-9604 is hydrophobic and will gel into a cloudy mess if you mix it with bacteriostatic water alone. This is one of the most common mistakes with this peptide. Acetic acid prevents it.
✅ YES — I ALREADY KNOW THIS. LET'S MOVE ON. ❓ NO — TELL ME WHY I NEED TO WORRY
📖 THE SCIENCE
What AOD-9604 actually is
AOD-9604 is a 16-amino-acid synthetic peptide derived from the C-terminus of human growth hormone. Its sequence is Tyr-Leu-Arg-Ile-Val-Gln-Cys-Arg-Ser-Val-Glu-Gly-Ser-Cys-Gly-Phe. About 45% of those residues are strongly hydrophobic — that's extremely high for a peptide this short.
Why BAC water alone causes gelling
Hydrophobic amino acids repel water. When you mix AOD with plain water, the peptide molecules cluster together to minimize water exposure, the chains stack, and the solution forms a cloudy gel or precipitate. The two cysteine residues can also cross-link with adjacent molecules, locking the aggregate in place.
Why acetic acid fixes it (3 mechanisms)
1. Same-charge repulsion. Acid protonates the peptide, making every molecule positively charged. Like charges repel — no clustering.

2. Weakened hydrophobic aggregation. Protonated surfaces are more polar, neutralizing the "oily patches."

3. Slowed disulfide bonding. Acid prevents the cysteine residues from cross-linking between molecules — the peptide stays monomeric.
The protocol
For a 10mg vial: 1 mL of 0.6–1% acetic acid + 2 mL BAC water (3 mL total).
Tilt the vial, pour slowly down the inside wall, do not shake, let the powder dissolve on its own. Preload your cartridges/syringes immediately — every future disturbance can re-aggregate a hydrophobic peptide.
🛒 Where to buy the right acetic acid
DO NOT USE: household vinegar, wood vinegar, apple cider vinegar, glacial / industrial-grade acetic acid, or anything from a grocery/cleaning aisle. These are the wrong concentration, not sterile, or contain contaminants.
WHAT TO BUY: Pre-diluted "sterile 0.6% acetic acid solution" (sometimes labeled "acetic acid water" or "AOD reconstitution solution"). 30 mL vial with rubber septum, ready-to-use. $15–25.
RECOMMENDED SUPPLIERS: Particle Peptides · Amino Asylum · Modern Amino · Limitless Life Nootropics · Simple Peptide · SwissChems. Search "acetic acid water" or "AOD reconstitution solution".
Label should say: sterile · 0.6% acetic acid · peptide / research grade · 30 mL multi-dose vial.
💡 Why the Apex Pen matters here: You reconstitute ONCE, preload the cartridge ONCE, then every dose is pulled without disturbing the solution. That's the whole point of pen-based dosing for hydrophobic peptides.
